When corrupted or non-image files, such as GitHub \"Not Found\" pages saved as `.png`, are read, they can cause \"Could not process image\" errors and crash the AI solver process. When the file command shows \"HTML\", \"text\", or \"ASCII text\", the download failed, so do not call Read on that file. Instead: (1) when images are from GitHub issues or PRs, such as URLs containing \"github.com/user-attachments\", retry with: curl -L -H \"Authorization: token \$(gh auth token)\" -o <filename> \"<url>\" (2) when the retry still fails, skip the image and note that it was unavailable.

[2026-05-20T16:35:41.724Z] [INFO]    - When fetching lists from GitHub API, use the --paginate flag to ensure all results are returned (GitHub returns max 30 per page by default).
[2026-05-20T16:35:41.724Z] [INFO]    - When listing PR review comments (inline code comments), use gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/pulls/NUMBER/comments --paginate.
[2026-05-20T16:35:41.724Z] [INFO]    - When listing PR conversation comments, use gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/issues/NUMBER/comments --paginate.
[2026-05-20T16:35:41.724Z] [INFO]    - When listing PR reviews, use gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/pulls/NUMBER/reviews --paginate.
[2026-05-20T16:35:41.724Z] [INFO]    - When listing issue comments, use gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/issues/NUMBER/comments --paginate.
[2026-05-20T16:35:41.724Z] [INFO]    - When adding PR comment, use gh pr comment NUMBER --body \"text\" --repo OWNER/REPO.
[2026-05-20T16:35:41.724Z] [INFO]    - When adding issue comment, use gh issue comment NUMBER --body \"text\" --repo OWNER/REPO.
[2026-05-20T16:35:41.724Z] [INFO]    - When viewing PR details, use gh pr view NUMBER --repo OWNER/REPO.
[2026-05-20T16:35:41.724Z] [INFO]    - When filtering with jq, use gh api repos/\${owner}/\${repo}/pulls/\${prNumber}/comments --paginate --jq 'reverse | .[0:5]'.
[2026-05-20T16:35:41.724Z] [INFO]
[2026-05-20T16:35:41.724Z] [INFO] Playwright MCP usage (browser automation via mcp__playwright__* tools).
[2026-05-20T16:35:41.724Z] [INFO]    - When you develop frontend web applications (HTML, CSS, JavaScript, React, Vue, Angular, etc.), use Playwright MCP tools to test the UI in a real browser.
[2026-05-20T16:35:41.724Z] [INFO]    - When WebFetch tool fails to retrieve expected content (e.g., returns empty content, JavaScript-rendered pages, or login-protected pages), use Playwright MCP tools (browser_navigate, browser_snapshot) as a fallback for web browsing.
[2026-05-20T16:35:41.724Z] [INFO]    - When WebSearch tool fails or returns insufficient results, use Playwright MCP tools (browser_navigate, browser_snapshot) as a fallback for internet search.
[2026-05-20T16:35:41.724Z] [INFO]    - When you need to interact with dynamic web pages that require JavaScript execution, use Playwright MCP tools.
[2026-05-20T16:35:41.724Z] [INFO]    - When you need to visually verify how a web page looks or take screenshots, use browser_take_screenshot from Playwright MCP.
[2026-05-20T16:35:41.724Z] [INFO]    - When you need to fill forms, click buttons, or perform user interactions on web pages, use Playwright MCP tools (browser_click, browser_type, browser_fill_form).
[2026-05-20T16:35:41.724Z] [INFO]    - When you need to test responsive design or different viewport sizes, use browser_resize from Playwright MCP.
[2026-05-20T16:35:41.724Z] [INFO]    - When you finish using the browser, close it with browser_close to free resources.
[2026-05-20T16:35:41.724Z] [INFO]    - When reproducing UI bugs, use browser_take_screenshot to capture the problem state before implementing any fix.
[2026-05-20T16:35:41.724Z] [INFO]    - When fixing UI bugs, take before/after screenshots to provide visual evidence of the fix for human verification.
[2026-05-20T16:35:41.724Z] [INFO]    - When creating UI tests, save baseline screenshots to the repository for visual regression testing.
[2026-05-20T16:35:41.724Z] [INFO]    - When verifying UI fixes, compare screenshots to ensure the fix does not introduce unintended visual changes.
[2026-05-20T16:35:41.724Z] [INFO]
[2026-05-20T16:35:41.724Z] [INFO] Visual UI work and screenshots.
[2026-05-20T16:35:41.724Z] [INFO]    - When you work on visual UI changes (frontend, CSS, HTML, design), include a render or screenshot of the final result in the pull request description.
[2026-05-20T16:35:41.724Z] [INFO]    - When you need to show visual results, take a screenshot and save it to the repository (e.g., in a docs/screenshots/ or assets/ folder).

[2026-05-20T16:35:41.724Z] [INFO]    - When uploading images, commit them to the branch first, then reference them using the GitHub blob URL format with ?raw=true suffix (works for both public and private repositories).
[2026-05-20T16:35:41.724Z] [INFO]    - When the visual result is important for review, mention it explicitly in the pull request description with the embedded image.
[2026-05-20T16:35:41.724Z] [INFO]    - When fixing UI bugs, capture both the \"before\" (problem) and \"after\" (fixed) screenshots as evidence for human verification.
[2026-05-20T16:35:41.724Z] [INFO]    - When reporting UI bugs, include a screenshot of the problem state to enable visual verification of the fix.
[2026-05-20T16:35:41.724Z] [INFO]    - When the fix is visual, include side-by-side or sequential comparison of before/after states in the PR description.
[2026-05-20T16:35:41.724Z] [INFO]    - When possible, create automated visual regression tests to prevent the UI bug from recurring." | jq -c .)

[2026-05-20T16:35:41.725Z] [INFO]    - When you read the issue, read all details and comments thoroughly.
[2026-05-20T16:35:41.725Z] [INFO]    - When you see screenshots or images in issue descriptions, pull request descriptions, comments, or discussions, download the image to a local file first, then use the Read tool to view and analyze it. Before reading downloaded images with the Read tool, verify that the file is a valid image rather than HTML by using a CLI tool such as the 'file' command. When corrupted or non-image files, such as GitHub "Not Found" pages saved as `.png`, are read, they can cause "Could not process image" errors and crash the AI solver process. When the file command shows "HTML", "text", or "ASCII text", the download failed, so do not call Read on that file. Instead: (1) when images are from GitHub issues or PRs, such as URLs containing "github.com/user-attachments", retry with: curl -L -H "Authorization: token $(gh auth token)" -o <filename> "<url>" (2) when the retry still fails, skip the image and note that it was unavailable.

[2026-05-20T16:39:10.874Z] [INFO]       "content": "/*\n * Test for issue #2402: js/integram-table.js Выводить полный текст ошибки\n * при удалении колонки, а не просто \"Ошибка удаления: HTTP 400\".\n *\n * Reproduces the original bug (only \"HTTP 400\" surfaced) and verifies that the\n * fix returns and renders the full server error, including HTML links.\n */\n\nconst assert = require('assert');\n\n// --- Reproduce the relevant pieces of integram-table.js -------------------\n\nfunction makeTableLike() {\n    return {\n        getApiBase() { return 'https://example.test/db'; },\n        getServerError(result) {\n            if (Array.isArray(result)) {\n                return (result[0] && result[0].error) || null;\n            }\n            return result.error || null;\n        },\n    };\n}\n\n// New (fixed) implementation extracted from js/integram-table.js\nasync function deleteColumnFixed(colId, forced) {\n    const apiBase = this.getApiBase();\n    try {\n        const params = new URLSearchParams();\n        if (forced) params.append('forced', '1');\n\n        const resp = await fetch(`${apiBase}/_d_del_req/${colId}?JSON`, {\n            method: 'POST',\n            headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/x-www-form-urlencoded' },\n            body: params.toString(),\n        });\n        const responseText = await resp.text();\n        let result = null;\n        try { result = JSON.parse(responseText); } catch (_) { /* not JSON */ }\n        const serverError = result ? this.getServerError(result) : null;\n        if (!resp.ok) {\n            const errMsg = serverError || responseText || `HTTP ${resp.status}`;\n            return { success: false, error: errMsg };\n        }\n        if (serverError) {\n            return { success: false, hasData: true, error: serverError };\n        }\n        return { success: true };\n    } catch (err) {\n        return { success: false, error: err.message };\n    }\n}\n\n// Old (buggy) implementation kept for the reproduction part of the test\nasync function deleteColumnOld(colId, forced) {\n    const apiBase = this.getApiBase();\n    try {\n        const params = new URLSearchParams();\n        if (forced) params.append('forced', '1');\n\n        const resp = await fetch(`${apiBase}/_d_del_req/${colId}?JSON`, {\n            method: 'POST',\n            headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/x-www-form-urlencoded' },\n            body: params.toString(),\n        });\n        if (!resp.ok) return { success: false, error: `HTTP ${resp.status}` };\n        const result = await resp.json();\n        if (Array.isArray(result) && result[0] && result[0].error) {\n            return { success: false, hasData: true, error: result[0].error };\n        }\n        return { success: true };\n    } catch (err) {\n        return { success: false, error: err.message };\n    }\n}\n\n// --- Mock fetch -----------------------------------------------------------\n\nfunction mockFetchHttp400WithJsonError(body) {\n    return async () => ({\n        ok: false,\n        status: 400,\n        statusText: 'Bad Request',\n        async text() { return JSON.stringify(body); },\n        async json() { return body; },\n    });\n}\n\nfunction mockFetchOkWithError(body) {\n    return async () => ({\n        ok: true,\n        status: 200,\n        statusText: 'OK',\n        async text() { return JSON.stringify(body); },\n        async json() { return body; },\n    });\n}\n\nfunction mockFetchOkSuccess() {\n    return async () => ({\n        ok: true,\n        status: 200,\n        statusText: 'OK',\n        async text() { return JSON.stringify({ success: true }); },\n        async json() { return { success: true }; },\n    });\n}\n\n// --- Sanitizer copy from js/integram-table.js -----------------------------\n\nfunction sanitizeInlineMessageHtml(html) {\n    if (html === null || html === undefined) return '';\n    const str = String(html);\n    const placeholderPrefix = '__SAFE_ANCHOR__';\n    const safeAnchors = [];\n    const withAnchorPlaceholders = str.replace(\n        /<a\\b[^>]*href\\s*=\\s*([\"'])(.*?)\\1[^>]*>(.*?)<\\/a>/gi,\n        (match, quote, href, text) => {\n            const trimmedHref = String(href || '').trim();\n            const trimmedText = String(text || '').trim();\n            if (!trimmedText) return match;\n            if (!/^(https?:\\/\\/|\\/)/i.test(trimmedHref)) return match;\n            if (/^\\s*javascript:/i.test(trimmedHref)) return match;\n            const safeHref = trimmedHref\n                .replace(/&/g, '&amp;').replace(/\"/g, '&quot;')\n                .replace(/</g, '&lt;').replace(/>/g, '&gt;');\n            const safeText = trimmedText\n                .replace(/&/g, '&amp;').replace(/</g, '&lt;')\n                .replace(/>/g, '&gt;').replace(/\"/g, '&quot;')\n                .replace(/'/g, '&#039;');\n            const anchorHtml = `<a href=\"${safeHref}\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\">${safeText}</a>`;\n            const placeholder = `${placeholderPrefix}${safeAnchors.length}__`;\n            safeAnchors.push(anchorHtml);\n            return placeholder;\n        }\n    );\n    let escaped = withAnchorPlaceholders\n        .replace(/&/g, '&amp;').replace(/</g, '&lt;')\n        .replace(/>/g, '&gt;').replace(/\"/g, '&quot;')\n        .replace(/'/g, '&#039;');\n    escaped = escaped.replace(/&lt;br\\s*\\/?&gt;/gi, '<br>');\n    safeAnchors.forEach((anchorHtml, index) => {\n        const placeholder = `${placeholderPrefix}${index}__`;\n        escaped = escaped.replace(placeholder, anchorHtml);\n    });\n    return escaped;\n}\n\n// --- Tests ----------------------------------------------------------------\n\nconst ISSUE_BODY = [{\n    error: 'Этот реквизит используется в <a href=\"/sportzania/object/22/?F_28=6265\">отчетах</a> или <a href=\"/sportzania/object/42/?F_116=6265\">ролях</a>!',\n}];\n\nasync function testReproduceOriginalBug() {\n    global.fetch = mockFetchHttp400WithJsonError(ISSUE_BODY);\n    const tbl = makeTableLike();\n    const result = await deleteColumnOld.call(tbl, 6265, false);\n    assert.strictEqual(result.success, false);\n    assert.strictEqual(result.error, 'HTTP 400',\n        'Old code surfaces only \"HTTP 400\" (the bug from the issue)');\n    console.log('PASS reproduces issue #2402: old code returns \"HTTP 400\"');\n}\n\nasync function testFixSurfacesFullError() {\n    global.fetch = mockFetchHttp400WithJsonError(ISSUE_BODY);\n    const tbl = makeTableLike();\n    const result = await deleteColumnFixed.call(tbl, 6265, false);\n    assert.strictEqual(result.success, false);\n    assert.notStrictEqual(result.error, 'HTTP 400');\n    assert.ok(result.error.includes('Этот реквизит используется в'),\n        'Fixed code surfaces the full server error message');\n    assert.ok(result.error.includes('href=\"/sportzania/object/22/?F_28=6265\"'));\n    assert.ok(result.error.includes('href=\"/sportzania/object/42/?F_116=6265\"'));\n    console.log('PASS fix surfaces full server error message including links');\n}\n\nasync function testFixStillHandlesOkResponseWithError() {\n    // When server returns 200 OK but body contains an error (column has data)\n    global.fetch = mockFetchOkWithError(ISSUE_BODY);\n    const tbl = makeTableLike();\n    const result = await deleteColumnFixed.call(tbl, 6265, false);\n    assert.strictEqual(result.success, false);\n    assert.strictEqual(result.hasData, true,\n        'Existing hasData flow is preserved for 200 OK responses with error body');\n    assert.ok(result.error.includes('Этот реквизит используется в'));\n    console.log('PASS fix preserves hasData=true behavior for 200 responses with error');\n}\n\nasync function testFixHandlesSuccess() {\n    global.fetch = mockFetchOkSuccess();\n    const tbl = makeTableLike();\n    const result = await deleteColumnFixed.call(tbl, 6265, false);\n    assert.strictEqual(result.success, true);\n    console.log('PASS fix preserves success path');\n}\n\nasync function testFixFallsBackWhenBodyMissing() {\n    global.fetch = async () => ({\n        ok: false, status: 500, statusText: 'Internal Server Error',\n        async text() { return ''; },\n        async json() { throw new Error('no body'); },\n On success returns {id, obj, next_act, args, warnings}.\n         * @returns {Promise<{success: boolean, error?: string}>}\n         */\n        async deleteTable(tableId) {\n            const apiBase = this.getApiBase();\n            try {\n                const params = new URLSearchParams();\n                if (typeof xsrf !== 'undefined') params.append('_xsrf', xsrf);\n\n                const resp = await fetch(`${apiBase}/_d_del/${tableId}?JSON`, {\n                    method: 'POST',\n                    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/x-www-form-urlencoded' },\n                    body: params.toString()\n                });\n                // Issue #2402: parse response body even when HTTP status is not OK\n                // so the user sees the actual error instead of a generic \"HTTP 400\".\n                const responseText = await resp.text();\n                let result = null;\n                try { result = JSON.parse(responseText); } catch (_) { /* not JSON */ }\n                if (!resp.ok) {\n                    const serverError = result ? this.getServerError(result) : null;\n                    return { success: false, error: serverError || responseText || `HTTP ${resp.status}` };\n                }\n                if (result && result.id) return { success: true };\n                const err = (result && this.getServerError(result)) || 'Неизвестная ошибка';\n                return { success: false, error: err };\n            } catch (err) {\n                return { success: false, error: err.message };\n            }\n        }",
[2026-05-20T16:39:58.149Z] [INFO]           "new_string": "        /**\n         * Delete a table via API (issue #1932).\n         * Uses _d_del/{tableId}?JSON. On success returns {id, obj, next_act, args, warnings}.\n         *\n         * Issue #2746: if the table is referenced by another column (metadata\n         * exposes a \"referenced\" id), the server refuses to delete it. Fetch\n         * fresh metadata, delete each reference first via _d_del/{referenced},\n         * then delete the table itself. Surface the full server error on\n         * failure (no truncation, no generic \"HTTP 400\").\n         *\n         * @returns {Promise<{success: boolean, error?: string}>}\n         */\n        async deleteTable(tableId) {\n            const apiBase = this.getApiBase();\n            try {\n                // Issue #2746: remove any reference columns pointing to this table\n                // before deleting the table itself. Bypass the cache so we see\n                // the current server-side state.\n                const refResult = await this.deleteTableReferences(tableId);\n                if (!refResult.success) {\n                    return refResult;\n                }\n\n                const params = new URLSearchParams();\n                if (typeof xsrf !== 'undefined') params.append('_xsrf', xsrf);\n\n                const resp = await fetch(`${apiBase}/_d_del/${tableId}?JSON`, {\n                    method: 'POST',\n                    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/x-www-form-urlencoded' },\n                    body: params.toString()\n                });\n                // Issue #2402: parse response body even when HTTP status is not OK\n                // so the user sees the actual error instead of a generic \"HTTP 400\".\n                const responseText = await resp.text();\n                let result = null;\n                try { result = JSON.parse(responseText); } catch (_) { /* not JSON */ }\n                if (!resp.ok) {\n                    const serverError = result ? this.getServerError(result) : null;\n                    return { success: false, error: serverError || responseText || `HTTP ${resp.status}` };\n                }\n                if (result && result.id) return { success: true };\n                // Issue #2746: surface the raw response body when the JSON shape\n                // is unexpected, so the user sees the whole server message\n                // rather than the generic \"Неизвестная ошибка\".\n                const err = (result && this.getServerError(result)) || responseText || 'Неизвестная ошибка';\n                return { success: false, error: err };\n            } catch (err) {\n                return { success: false, error: err.message };\n            }\n        }\n\n        /**\n         * Delete every column that references the given table (issue #2746).\n         * The server marks such tables in metadata with a \"referenced\" field\n         * holding the requisite id; deleting that requisite removes the link.\n         * Repeats until metadata reports no more references or the request\n         * fails. Returns the full server error on failure.\n         *\n         * @returns {Promise<{success: boolean, error?: string}>}\n         */\n        async deleteTableReferences(tableId) {\n            const apiBase = this.getApiBase();\n            const seen = new Set();\n            // Cap iterations to avoid a runaway loop if the server keeps\n            // returning the same referenced id (it would also be caught by\n            // `seen`, but the cap is a defensive backstop).\n            for (let i = 0; i < 32; i++) {\n                let metadata;\n                try {\n                    const resp = await fetch(`${apiBase}/metadata/${tableId}`);\n                    if (!resp.ok) {\n                        // Can't inspect references; let the table-delete call\n                        // surface whatever error the server returns.\n                        return { success: true };\n                    }\n                    const text = await resp.text();\n                    try { metadata = JSON.parse(text); } catch (_) { return { success: true }; }\n                } catch (_) {\n                    return { success: true };\n                }\n                if (Array.isArray(metadata)) metadata = metadata[0];\n                if (!metadata) return { success: true };\n                const referenced = metadata.referenced;\n                if (!referenced) return { success: true };\n\n                const refIds = Array.isArray(referenced) ? referenced : [referenced];\n                let removedAny = false;\n                for (const rawRefId of refIds) {\n                    const refId = String(rawRefId || '').trim();\n                    if (!refId || seen.has(refId)) continue;\n                    seen.add(refId);\n                    const delResult = await this.deleteReferenceRequisite(refId);\n                    if (!delResult.success) {\n                        return {\n                            success: false,\n                            error: `Не удалось удалить ссылку ${refId}: ${delResult.error}`,\n                        };\n                    }\n                    removedAny = true;\n                }\n                if (!removedAny) {\n                    // Nothing new to remove this iteration; either we already\n                    // processed every reference or the metadata still reports\n                    // ones we cannot resolve. [2026-05-20T16:39:58.210Z] [INFO]     "newString": "        /**\n         * Delete a table via API (issue #1932).\n         * Uses _d_del/{tableId}?JSON. On success returns {id, obj, next_act, args, warnings}.\n         *\n         * Issue #2746: if the table is referenced by another column (metadata\n         * exposes a \"referenced\" id), the server refuses to delete it. Fetch\n         * fresh metadata, delete each reference first via _d_del/{referenced},\n         * then delete the table itself. Surface the full server error on\n         * failure (no truncation, no generic \"HTTP 400\").\n         *\n         * @returns {Promise<{success: boolean, error?: string}>}\n         */\n        async deleteTable(tableId) {\n            const apiBase = this.getApiBase();\n            try {\n                // Issue #2746: remove any reference columns pointing to this table\n                // before deleting the table itself. Bypass the cache so we see\n                // the current server-side state.\n                const refResult = await this.deleteTableReferences(tableId);\n                if (!refResult.success) {\n                    return refResult;\n                }\n\n                const params = new URLSearchParams();\n                if (typeof xsrf !== 'undefined') params.append('_xsrf', xsrf);\n\n                const resp = await fetch(`${apiBase}/_d_del/${tableId}?JSON`, {\n                    method: 'POST',\n                    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/x-www-form-urlencoded' },\n                    body: params.toString()\n                });\n                // Issue #2402: parse response body even when HTTP status is not OK\n                // so the user sees the actual error instead of a generic \"HTTP 400\".\n                const responseText = await resp.text();\n                let result = null;\n                try { result = JSON.parse(responseText); } catch (_) { /* not JSON */ }\n                if (!resp.ok) {\n                    const serverError = result ? referenced : [referenced];\n                let removedAny = false;\n                for (const rawRefId of refIds) {\n                    const refId = String(rawRefId || '').trim();\n                    if (!refId || seen.has(refId)) continue;\n                    seen.add(refId);\n                    const delResult = await this.deleteReferenceRequisite(refId);\n                    if (!delResult.success) {\n                        return {\n                            success: false,\n                            error: `Не удалось удалить ссылку ${refId}: ${delResult.error}`,\n                        };\n                    }\n                    removedAny = true;\n                }\n                if (!removedAny) {\n                    // Nothing new to remove this iteration; either we already\n                    // processed every reference or the metadata still reports\n                    // ones we cannot resolve.
